hawkish_phrases: inflation should stabilise at our two per cent target in the medium term
The economy remains resilient in a challenging global environment
credit standards for business loans tightened again
inflation could turn out to be higher if there were a persistent upward shift in energy prices
services inflation might come down later than expected
dovish_phrases: Inflation declined to 1.7 per cent in January, from 2.0 per cent in December
supportive effects of our past interest rate cuts are underpinning growth
Negotiated wage growth and forward-looking indicators point to a continued moderation in labour costs
Market rates have come down since our last meeting
Inflation could turn out to be lower if tariffs reduce demand for euro area exports by more than expected
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all three key rates unchanged at 2.00% (deposit facility), with a genuinely balanced outlook emphasizing both resilience and uncertainty, maintaining a data-dependent meeting-by-meeting approach without pre-committing to any rate path.
